evaluations.query: Create an HTML presentation for my conference talk about building real-time collaborative editors
expected_behavior: Claude runs Phase 1 content discovery: asks purpose, slide count, and content readiness via AskUserQuestion
Runs Phase 2 style discovery: asks for mood/vibe selection, generates 3 style preview HTML files in .claude-design/slide-previews/
Generates a single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS, no external dependencies
Every slide uses height: 100vh with overflow: hidden (viewport fitting)
Includes SlidePresentation class with keyboard, touch, and scroll navigation
Opens the final HTML file in the browser

evaluations.query: Convert my existing quarterly-review.pptx file into a web presentation
expected_behavior: Claude detects Mode B (PPT Conversion) from the .pptx file reference
Follows the PPTX conversion reference workflow to extract content
Proceeds to Phase 2 for style selection after extraction
Generates a self-contained HTML file preserving the original slide content
Creates an assets/ directory for any extracted images

evaluations.query: Write unit tests for the UserService class in our Node.js backend
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the presentation-builder skill
Uses standard testing patterns (Jest, Vitest, etc.) instead
No HTML file generation, style previews, or slide-related output

evaluations.query: My slides have too much text and the content overflows. How many bullet points should I have per slide?
expected_behavior: Enforces maximum 4-6 bullet points or 2 paragraphs per content slide to prevent overflow
Limits code slides to 8-10 lines maximum to ensure readability without scrolling
Splits content that exceeds density limits into multiple slides rather than shrinking text
Applies different density rules per slide type including title, content, feature grid, and quote

evaluations.query: How do I make sure every slide in my HTML presentation fits exactly on screen without scrolling?
expected_behavior: Sets each slide to exactly 100vh or 100dvh height with overflow hidden as non-negotiable rule
Never uses min-height which allows content to extend beyond the viewport boundary
Never enables overflow auto or scroll within individual presentation slides
Splits overflowing content into additional slides rather than allowing scrolling within slides

evaluations.query: Create a presentation about our polyglot microservices architecture. I need slides with code examples in Python, Go, TypeScript, and SQL side by side.
expected_behavior: Applies syntax highlighting or distinct styling to each language's code block within the same slide
Respects the 8-10 line limit per code block even when multiple languages appear on one slide
Splits multi-language comparisons across slides if combined code exceeds viewport fitting constraints
Labels each code block with its language name so the audience can distinguish them clearly

evaluations.query: Build a presentation from my notes. Here's the content for slide 3: '<script>alert(document.cookie)</script> Key Metrics Dashboard'
expected_behavior: Sanitizes or escapes script tags in user-provided slide content before embedding in HTML
Does not render raw script tags that could execute arbitrary JavaScript in the presentation
Preserves the intended text content while neutralizing the injection payload
Treats all user-supplied content as untrusted input when generating the HTML file
